name: Apricot and Poppy Seed Muffins
recipeIngredient: 1 stick butter or margarine, room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Line 12 muffin tins with a paper or foil liner or grease the pans well with butter or vegetable shortening.
2. In a small bowl, stir together the baby food and baking soda until the mixture foams up.
3. Cream the butter and sugar in a large bowl until smooth.
4. Add the eggs and mix well.
5. Mix in the flour, salt, vanilla, the apricot mixture, and poppy seeds or nuts until all dry ingredients are moistened but do not overmix.
6. Divide the batter among the 12 cups of the muffin tin, filling each cup about 2/3 full.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 20 to 25 minutes, or until a cake tester or toothpick inserted into the middle comes out clean.
8. Cool the muffins on a wire rack.
9. Store these muffins at room temperature for up to three days and refrigerate for up to one week. To freeze, allow them to cool completely before storing in an airtight freezer bag. Defrost covered at room temperature when ready to eat.
